# UC-22.13.29 · Security Patch Evaluation Completion Tracking

## Description

We track that each published critical patch for BES assets received a documented evaluation record within policy time.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc, patch and sourcetypes cip:patch_evaluation, tenable:plugin`, wsus:summary. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Ingest patch catalog with release dates. (2) Require evaluation HEC event per KB. (3) Alert before internal evaluation SLA. (4) Export for CIP-007 evidence binders. Indexes required: `index=grc`, `index=patch`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cip:patch_evaluation`, `sourcetype=tenable:plugin``, `sourcetype=wsus:summary`, `sourcetype=wsus:summary``; Lookups: `bes_cyber_asset_inventory`, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=patch` `sourcetype=wsus:summary` or `sourcetype=tenable:plugin`, `patch_evaluation_register.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest patch catalog with release dates. (2) Require evaluation HEC event per KB. (3) Alert before internal evaluation SLA. (4) Export for CIP-007 evidence binders.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=patch sourcetype=wsus:summary earliest=-35d classification="Critical"
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv ComputerName AS host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| join type=left kb_id [
    search index=grc sourcetype=cip:patch_evaluation kb_id=* earliest=-35d
    | stats latest(evaluated_at) as evaluated_at by kb_id
  ]
| where isnull(evaluated_at)
| stats dc(host) as hosts_missing_eval by kb_id, title
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Patch Evaluation Completion Tracking (CIP-007-6 R2 Part 2.1)** — We track that each published critical patch for BES assets received a documented evaluation record within policy time.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=patch` `sourcetype=wsus:summary` or `sourcetype=tenable:plugin`, `patch_evaluation_register.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: patch; **sourcetype**: wsus:summary. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=patch, sourcetype=wsus:summary, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(bes_system)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(evaluated_at)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **kb_id, title**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.

Output fields: evaluated_at, hosts_missing_eval, kb_id, title.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP firewall log, Windows Security, or the asset register—whichever the search is built to mirror. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unevaluated KBs), Single value (count), Time chart (evaluations)

## SPL

```spl
index=patch sourcetype=wsus:summary earliest=-35d classification="Critical"
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv ComputerName AS host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| join type=left kb_id [
    search index=grc sourcetype=cip:patch_evaluation kb_id=* earliest=-35d
    | stats latest(evaluated_at) as evaluated_at by kb_id
  ]
| where isnull(evaluated_at)
| stats dc(host) as hosts_missing_eval by kb_id, title
```

## Visualization

Table (unevaluated KBs), Single value (count), Time chart (evaluations). Key fields: `evaluated_at`, `hosts_missing_eval`, `kb_id`, `title`.
